content.content:
1→# k3rn.labs — Architecture Globale du Repository
     2→
     3→## Statut du document
     4→- **Type** : README d’architecture globale (document racine)
     5→- **Niveau** : Structurant / Contractuel (niveau dépôt)
     6→- **Audience** : Fondateurs, architectes, experts, partenaires techniques
     7→- **Rôle** : Définir la structure du repository et les responsabilités de chaque dossier
     8→- **Principe clé** : Séparation stricte entre capacités, raisonnement, canon et exploration
     9→
    10→---
    11→
    12→## 1. Principe d’architecture
    13→
    14→Le repository k3rn.labs est organisé selon une règle non négociable :
    15→
    16→> **Aucune ambiguïté entre ce qui CAPABLE, ce qui DÉCIDE, ce qui FAIT FOI et ce qui EXPLORE.**
    17→
    18→Chaque dossier a :
    19→- un rôle unique,
    20→- un statut explicite,
    21→- des règles de gouvernance claires.
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## 2. Arborescence canonique
    26→
    27→```text
    28→/
    29→├── agents/        # Skill Operating System (vendored)
    30→├── documents/     # Canon produit & business (STABLE)
    31→├── references/    # Sources externes & benchmarks
    32→├── workspace/     # Raisonnement, experts, LABS, décisions
    33→└── prototype/     # Exploration & simulation
    34→```
    35→
    36→Cette arborescence est **définitive**.
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## 3. Dossier `agents/` — SKILL OPERATING SYSTEM (vendored)
    41→
    42→### Rôle
    43→`agents/` embarque un **repository externe versionné** fournissant plus de
    44→600 skills agentiques réutilisables pour des agents IA
    45→(développement, architecture, data, sécurité, business, workflow, etc.).
    46→
    47→Il s’agit d’un **Skill Operating System autonome**, intégré à k3rn.labs
    48→comme dépendance vendored.
    49→
    50→### Nature
    51→- Repository autonome
    52→- Versionné (Semantic Versioning)
    53→- Gouvernance explicite (Changelog, Contribution rules, Quality Bar)
    54→- Multi-plateforme (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Copilot, Antigravity, etc.)
    55→
    56→### Contenu structurant
    57→- `skills/` : un dossier par skill (`SKILL.md` + playbooks / ressources)
    58→- `CATALOG.md` : registre exhaustif des skills
    59→- `docs/` : bundles, guidelines, sécurité, onboarding
    60→- `bin/`, `lib/`, `data/`, `assets/` : tooling d’installation et d’exécution
    61→- `package.json` : scripts, version, métadonnées
    62→- `CHANGELOG.md` : historique versionné
    63→
    64→### Statut
    65→- **STABLE dans sa structure**
    66→- **ÉVOLUTIF dans son contenu**
    67→- **NON CONTRACTUEL au niveau produit**
    68→
    69→### Règles strictes
    70→- `agents/` ne définit **aucune règle métier**
    71→- `agents/` ne prend **aucune décision**
    72→- Aucun contenu de `agents/` n’a autorité sur :
    73→  - la vision produit
    74→  - l’architecture du workspace
    75→  - les règles LABS
    76→- Les skills sont :
    77→  - sélectionnés par des agents
    78→  - combinés contextuellement
    79→  - toujours subordonnés au canon produit
    80→
    81→### Hors périmètre explicite
    82→- Orchestration runtime des agents
    83→- Sélection automatique des skills
    84→- Priorisation / scoring
    85→- Sécurité d’exécution runtime
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→## 4. Dossier `documents/` — CANON PRODUIT & BUSINESS
    90→
    91→### Rôle
    92→`documents/` contient **l’ensemble des documents de référence officiels** du projet.
    93→Ces documents font foi.
    94→
    95→Ils définissent :
    96→- la vision,
    97→- les règles,
    98→- les choix structurants,
    99→- les engagements produit et business.
   100→
   101→### Contenu typique
   102→- Product Vision Document (PVD)
   103→- Product Requirements Document (PRD)
   104→- Product Roadmap
   105→- Technical Architecture Document (TAD)
   106→- API Documentation
   107→- Data Model Specification
   108→- Business Model Canvas
   109→- Go-To-Market Strategy
   110→- Financial Projections
   111→- Investor Pitch Deck
   112→
   113→### Statut
   114→- **STABLE**
   115→- Versionné
   116→- Toute modification doit être :
   117→  - intentionnelle,
   118→  - tracée,
   119→  - justifiée.
   120→
   121→### Règles
   122→- Aucun brouillon
   123→- Aucune exploration
   124→- Aucun prototype
   125→- Pas de duplication avec `workspace/`
   126→
   127→### Hors périmètre
   128→- Implémentation technique détaillée
   129→- UI / design pixel-level
   130→- Orchestration IA runtime
   131→
   132→---
   133→
   134→## 5. Dossier `references/` — SUPPORT FACTUEL
   135→
   136→### Rôle
   137→`references/` centralise les **sources externes** servant à étayer le raisonnement :
   138→- articles,
   139→- études,
   140→- benchmarks,
   141→- frameworks,
   142→- inspirations.
   143→
   144→### Statut
   145→- **STABLE**
   146→- **NON CONTRACTUEL**
   147→
   148→### Règles
   149→- Aucune décision ne naît ici
   150→- Toute décision doit être reformulée et figée dans :
   151→  - `documents/`
   152→  - ou `workspace/`
   153→
   154→---
   155→
   156→## 6. Dossier `workspace/` — ZONE OPÉRATIONNELLE
   157→
   158→### Rôle
   159→`workspace/` est l’espace de **travail cognitif actif** :
   160→- raisonnement,
   161→- structuration,
   162→- arbitrage,
   163→- décisions préparatoires.
   164→
   165→### Contenu structurant
   166→- `experts/` : rôles cognitifs (experts world-class)
   167→- `laboratoires/` : LABS (états de raisonnement)
   168→- `executive/` :
   169→  - objets & interactions
   170→  - règles contractuelles du workspace
   171→  - mémos décisionnels
   172→
   173→### Statut
   174→- **EN COURS par nature**
   175→- Évolutif
   176→- Non contractuel tant que non promu
   177→
   178→### Règles
   179→- Rien n’est canon tant que non figé dans `documents/`
   180→- Toute logique stabilisée doit remonter vers le canon
   181→
   182→### Hors périmètre
   183→- UI finale
   184→- Code applicatif
   185→- Déploiement
   186→
   187→---
   188→
   189→## 7. Dossier `prototype/` — EXPLORATION & SIMULATION
   190→
   191→### Rôle
   192→Zone de **projection visuelle ou fonctionnelle** :
   193→- maquettes,
   194→- POC,
   195→- simulations UX,
   196→- parcours illustratifs.
   197→
   198→### Statut
   199→- **INSTABLE**
   200→- **JETABLE**
   201→- Non contractuel
   202→
   203→### Règles
   204→- Aucun prototype ne fait foi
   205→- Aucun prototype ne décide
   206→- Aucun prototype ne contredit le canon
   207→
   208→---
   209→
   210→## 8. Règles de circulation entre dossiers
   211→
   212→```text
   213→references/  →  workspace/  →  documents/
   214→                    ↑
   215→               prototype/
   216→```
   217→
   218→- `references/` alimente la réflexion
   219→- `workspace/` structure le raisonnement
   220→- `documents/` fige la vérité
   221→- `prototype/` illustre sans jamais décider
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## 9. Principe fondamental
   226→
   227→> **La clarté structurelle prime sur la vitesse.**
   228→
   229→Ce repository est conçu pour éviter :
   230→- les dérives de périmètre,
   231→- les décisions implicites,
   232→- la confusion entre exploration et engagement.
   233→
   234→---
   235→
   236→**Fin du README_ARCHITECTURE_GLOBALE.md**
   237→

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me provide a detailed report.

---

## Project Exploration Report: K3RN.LABS Platform

### 1. Project Type & Framework

**Project Name:** k3rn-platform (part of k3rn.labs)  
**Framework:** Next.js (v16.1.6)  
**Language:** TypeScript (React 19.2.3)  
**Architecture:** Full-stack web application with App Router  
**Package Manager:** npm  
**UI Components:** shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS (v4)  
**Database/Backend:** Supabase with Next.js SSR  

**Key Dependencies:**
- Next.js 16.1.6 (React 19.2.3)
- Tailwind CSS 4 with PostCSS
- Lucide React for icons
- Zustand for state management
- XState for state machines
- Anthropic SDK & OpenAI for AI features
- Framer Motion for animations
- Sonner for toast notifications

### 2. Project Detail Overlay Component

**Primary File:** `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/project-detail-overlay.tsx`

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Status:** Full client-side component (use client)
- **Size:** 430 lines of code
- **Purpose:** Modal overlay displaying comprehensive project/dossier details with save functionality
- **Language:** French (UI is in French)

content.thinking:
Now I can see the bug. Let me analyze the data flow:

1. In `workspace/page.tsx`, when a user clicks on a project card (line 246-249), `detailProject` is set to a **snapshot** of the project object at that moment.

2. In `handleSaveProject` (line 132-138), the save updates the `projects` state array and localStorage. However, it does NOT close the overlay (`setDetailOpen(false)` is not called, unlike archive/unarchive/delete handlers).

3. The key issue: `handleSaveProject` updates the `projects` array state, but it does NOT update `detailProject`. So after saving, the overlay still shows the old data, and the `detailProject` still has the old reference.
   
4. The overlay component reads from `editTitle` and `editDescription` state which are initialized from the `project` prop via useEffect (line 114-119). The `project` prop is `detailProject` from the parent, so when `detailProject` doesn't update, the overlay never re-initializes with the saved values.

So the actual save flow is: user edits title/description in the overlay, clicks "Sauvegarder", the parent's `handleSaveProject` runs and updates the `projects` array and localStorage, but `detailProject` stays unchanged. The overlay remains open with the edited values from local state, giving no visual feedback that anything was saved.

The real issue might be with how demo projects are initialized - when `storedProjects.length === 0`, mock projects are set...

Actually, wait. The demo projects get set in state but never saved to localStorage initially. When `handleSaveProject` runs, it should save them to localStorage for the first time. But from the user's perspective, the most likely problem is the lack of visual feedback - after clicking save, nothing visibly changes, so they think it failed.

There's also a potential issue with how the detail overlay gets its data. When the user reopens the overlay after saving, it pulls from `filteredProjects` which derives from the updated `projects` state, so the data should be fresh. Let me trace through the click handler that opens the detail view to confirm the data flow is working correctly. The dependency array only triggers when `project` changes, but after saving, the `detailProject` state stays stale while `projects` gets updated—so the overlay keeps showing the edited values locally, but if the user closes and reopens, it should pull the fresh data from the updated array. The real issue might be that `detailProject` isn't getting refreshed properly when the same card is clicked again after a save. I see the issue now—the overlay header displays the title from the `project` prop rather than the edited `editTitle` state, so even after saving, the heading still shows the old value while the input field shows the new one. The data is actually being persisted correctly to localStorage and state, but there's a disconnect between what the header displays and what's been edited. but the user specifically says the save button "doesn't save" (ne sauvegarde pas). Let me look even more carefully...

Actually, I just realized something. The save function does update state and persist to localStorage, so the save mechanism itself is working. But there's no visual feedback to the user, and the overlay header still shows stale data since `detailProject` isn't being refreshed.

Wait, I think I found the actual bug. When the app first loads, if there are no stored projects, it initializes with mock data but never saves that to localStorage...

Examining the save handler more closely...

The `handleSaveProject` function takes the project ID and updates object, maps over the projects array to find the matching one, spreads the updates into it, updates state, and persists to localStorage. This should definitely work. I'm wondering if there's a stale closure issue where `projects` isn't being captured correctly, but that seems unlikely given how the function is structured. `handleSaveProject` gets recreated on every render without `useCallback`, so it always has access to the latest `projects` state. But I'm realizing the real issue might be something else entirely—the user probably isn't seeing any confirmation that their changes saved, and the overlay header is reading from a stale `detailProject` prop that doesn't reflect the updates. They might actually be saving successfully, but the UI isn't updating to show it. Let me look more closely at that header...

The header displays the old title and description from the prop, while the editable fields below show the new values—creating a confusing mismatch. But the bigger problem is likely that unlike `handleArchive` and `handleUnarchive`, which close the overlay after their actions, `handleSaveProject` doesn't close it or provide any visual feedback, so the user has no way to know if anything actually happened. So the data persists correctly when closing and reopening the overlay—the real problem is just the lack of visual feedback when saving. The user might be editing, hitting save, seeing nothing happen, and assuming it didn't work. Let me trace through the overlay initialization to see if there's something else going on with how the form gets populated. The core issue is that `handleSaveProject` updates the backend and localStorage but doesn't refresh the overlay UI—no toast notification, no visual confirmation, and the header still displays stale data. The cleanest solution is to update `detailProject` with the new values after saving succeeds, optionally closing the overlay or showing a toast for consistency with the archive and delete actions.

Now I'm reconsidering whether the save is actually working at all, since the user reported it's not saving. If the data truly isn't persisting to localStorage and state, then there's a deeper issue beyond just missing feedback.

Let me trace through what happens with demo projects specifically—they only load if localStorage is empty, so when a user edits and saves one, the `handleSaveProject` function should write it to localStorage and update state. On the next page load, that saved data should be retrieved from localStorage instead of reloading the demo projects. The changes should persist, but maybe there's a timing issue or the state isn't actually being updated when the save runs.

Actually, I think I've found the real problem: `handleSaveProject` updates the `projects` array in localStorage, but it never updates the `detailProject` state that's displayed in the overlay. So even though the save works internally, the user sees stale data in the overlay header and gets no visual confirmation that anything happened. That's why it feels like the save failed.

The fix is straightforward — I need to update `detailProject` after saving, and I should add a toast notification for feedback. Let me check if sonner is already set up in this workspace.

1→"use client"
     2→
     3→import * as React from "react"
     4→import { Avatar as AvatarPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
     5→
     6→import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
     7→
     8→function Avatar({
     9→  className,
    10→  size = "default",
    11→  ...props
    12→}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AvatarPrimitive.Root> & {
    13→  size?: "default" | "sm" | "lg"
    14→}) {
    15→  return (
    16→    <AvatarPrimitive.Root
    17→      data-slot="avatar"
    18→      data-size={size}
    19→      className={cn(
    20→        "group/avatar relative flex size-8 shrink-0 overflow-hidden rounded-none select-none data-[size=lg]:size-10 data-[size=sm]:size-6",
    21→        className
    22→      )}
    23→      {...props}
    24→    />
    25→  )
    26→}
    27→
    28→function AvatarImage({
    29→  className,
    30→  ...props
    31→}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AvatarPrimitive.Image>) {
    32→  return (
    33→    <AvatarPrimitive.Image
    34→      data-slot="avatar-image"
    35→      className={cn("aspect-square size-full", className)}
    36→      {...props}
    37→    />
    38→  )
    39→}
    40→
    41→function AvatarFallback({
    42→  className,
    43→  ...props
    44→}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AvatarPrimitive.Fallback>) {
    45→  return (
    46→    <AvatarPrimitive.Fallback
    47→      data-slot="avatar-fallback"
    48→      className={cn(
    49→        "bg-muted text-muted-foreground flex size-full items-center justify-center rounded-none text-sm group-data-[size=sm]/avatar:text-xs",
    50→        className
    51→      )}
    52→      {...props}
    53→    />
    54→  )
    55→}
    56→
    57→function AvatarBadge({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"span">) {
    58→  return (
    59→    <span
    60→      data-slot="avatar-badge"
    61→      className={cn(
    62→        "bg-primary text-primary-foreground ring-background absolute right-0 bottom-0 z-10 inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-none ring-2 select-none",
    63→        "group-data-[size=sm]/avatar:size-2 group-data-[size=sm]/avatar:[&>svg]:hidden",
    64→        "group-data-[size=default]/avatar:size-2.5 group-data-[size=default]/avatar:[&>svg]:size-2",
    65→        "group-data-[size=lg]/avatar:size-3 group-data-[size=lg]/avatar:[&>svg]:size-2",
    66→        className
    67→      )}
    68→      {...props}
    69→    />
    70→  )
    71→}
    72→
    73→function AvatarGroup({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
    74→  return (
    75→    <div
    76→      data-slot="avatar-group"
    77→      className={cn(
    78→        "*:data-[slot=avatar]:ring-background group/avatar-group flex -space-x-2 *:data-[slot=avatar]:ring-2",
    79→        className
    80→      )}
    81→      {...props}
    82→    />
    83→  )
    84→}
    85→
    86→function AvatarGroupCount({
    87→  className,
    88→  ...props
    89→}: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
    90→  return (
    91→    <div
    92→      data-slot="avatar-group-count"
    93→      className={cn(
    94→        "bg-muted text-muted-foreground ring-background relative flex size-8 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-none text-sm ring-2 group-has-data-[size=lg]/avatar-group:size-10 group-has-data-[size=sm]/avatar-group:size-6 [&>svg]:size-4 group-has-data-[size=lg]/avatar-group:[&>svg]:size-5 group-has-data-[size=sm]/avatar-group:[&>svg]:size-3",
    95→        className
    96→      )}
    97→      {...props}
    98→    />
    99→  )
   100→}

1→import * as React from "react"
     2→import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
     3→import { Slot } from "radix-ui"
     4→
     5→import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
     6→
     7→const badgeVariants = cva(
     8→  "inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-none border border-transparent px-2 py-0.5 text-xs font-medium w-fit whitespace-nowrap shrink-0 [&>svg]:size-3 gap-1 [&>svg]:pointer-events-none focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:ring-[3px] aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 aria-invalid:border-destructive transition-[color,box-shadow] overflow-hidden",
     9→  {
    10→    variants: {
    11→      variant: {
    12→        default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground [a&]:hover:bg-primary/90",
    13→        secondary:
    14→          "bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground [a&]:hover:bg-secondary/90",
    15→        destructive:
    16→          "bg-destructive text-white [a&]:hover:bg-destructive/90 focus-visible:ring-destructive/20 dark:focus-visible:ring-destructive/40 dark:bg-destructive/60",
    17→        outline:
    18→          "border-border text-foreground [a&]:hover:bg-accent [a&]:hover:text-accent-foreground",
    19→        ghost: "[a&]:hover:bg-accent [a&]:hover:text-accent-foreground",
    20→        link: "text-primary underline-offset-4 [a&]:hover:underline",
    21→      },
    22→    },
    23→    defaultVariants: {
    24→      variant: "default",
    25→    },
    26→  }
    27→)
    28→
    29→function Badge({
    30→  className,
    31→  variant = "default",
    32→  asChild = false,
    33→  ...props
    34→}: React.ComponentProps<"span"> &
    35→  VariantProps<typeof badgeVariants> & { asChild?: boolean }) {
    36→  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "span"
    37→
    38→  return (
    39→    <Comp
    40→      data-slot="badge"
    41→      data-variant={variant}
    42→      className={cn(badgeVariants({ variant }), className)}
    43→      {...props}
    44→    />
    45→  )
    46→}
    47→
    48→export { Badge, badgeVariants }
    49→

     2→import { updateSession } from '@/lib/supabase/middleware'
     3→
     4→export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
     5→    return await updateSession(request)
     6→}
     7→
     8→export const config = {
     9→    matcher: [
    10→        /*
    11→         * Match all request paths except for the ones starting with:
    12→         * - _next/static (static files)
    13→         * - _next/image (image optimization files)
    14→         * - favicon.ico (favicon file)
    15→         * Feel free to modify this pattern to include more paths.
    16→         */
    17→        '/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)',
    18→    ],
    19→}

     8→const Slider = React.forwardRef<
     9→    React.ElementRef<typeof SliderPrimitive.Root>,
    10→    React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof SliderPrimitive.Root>
    11→>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (

    13→        ref={ref}
    14→        className={cn(

    18→        {...props}

    21→            <SliderPrimitive.Range className="absolute h-full bg-primary" />
    22→        </SliderPrimitive.Track>
    23→        <SliderPrimitive.Thumb className="block h-5 w-5 rounded-none border-2 border-primary bg-background ring-offset-background transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50" />
    24→    </SliderPrimitive.Root>
    25→))

    27→
    28→export { Slider }

1→"use client"
     2→
     3→import * as React from "react"
     4→import { Switch as SwitchPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
     5→
     6→import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
     7→
     8→function Switch({
     9→  className,
    10→  size = "default",
    11→  ...props
    12→}: React.ComponentProps<typeof SwitchPrimitive.Root> & {
    13→  size?: "sm" | "default"
    14→}) {
    15→  return (
    16→    <SwitchPrimitive.Root
    17→      data-slot="switch"
    18→      data-size={size}
    19→      className={cn(
    20→        "peer data-[state=checked]:bg-primary data-[state=unchecked]:bg-input focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-ring/50 dark:data-[state=unchecked]:bg-input/80 group/switch inline-flex shrink-0 items-center rounded-none border border-transparent transition-all outline-none focus-visible:ring-[3px] disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 data-[size=default]:h-[1.15rem] data-[size=default]:w-8 data-[size=sm]:h-3.5 data-[size=sm]:w-6",
    21→        className
    22→      )}
    23→      {...props}
    24→    >
    25→      <SwitchPrimitive.Thumb
    26→        data-slot="switch-thumb"
    27→        className={cn(
    28→          "bg-background dark:data-[state=unchecked]:bg-foreground dark:data-[state=checked]:bg-primary-foreground pointer-events-none block rounded-none ring-0 transition-transform group-data-[size=default]/switch:size-4 group-data-[size=sm]/switch:size-3 data-[state=checked]:translate-x-[calc(100%-2px)] data-[state=unchecked]:translate-x-0"
    29→        )}
    30→      />
    31→    </SwitchPrimitive.Root>
    32→  )
    33→}
    34→
    35→export { Switch }
    36→

1→/**
     2→ * K3RN.LABS — Constantes canoniques pour l'analyse d'idées
     3→ * Source de vérité : Contrat Canonique Analyse Idée v1.1
     4→ */
     5→
     6→// ─── Axes canoniques ─────────────────────────────────────────────
     7→export const ANALYSIS_AXES = [
     8→  'problem',
     9→  'market',
    10→  'differentiation',
    11→  'feasibility',
    12→  'business',
    13→  'founder_fit',
    14→] as const;
    15→
    16→export type AnalysisAxis = (typeof ANALYSIS_AXES)[number];
    17→
    18→// ─── Pondérations canoniques (Section 4.2) ───────────────────────
    19→export const CANONICAL_WEIGHTS: Record<AnalysisAxis, number> = {
    20→  problem: 0.2,
    21→  market: 0.2,
    22→  differentiation: 0.15,
    23→  feasibility: 0.15,
    24→  business: 0.2,
    25→  founder_fit: 0.1,
    26→} as const;
    27→
    28→// ─── Axes critiques (Problème, Marché, Business) ─────────────────
    29→export const CRITICAL_AXES: AnalysisAxis[] = ['problem', 'market', 'business'];
    30→
    31→// ─── Seuils de décision (Section 5.1) ────────────────────────────
    32→export const DECISION_THRESHOLDS = {
    33→  KILL_SCORE: 30,
    34→  KILL_PROBLEM_MIN: 20,
    35→  PARK_MIN: 30,
    36→  PARK_MAX: 50,
    37→  PARK_CRITICAL_MIN: 25,
    38→  EXPLORE_MIN: 50,
    39→  EXPLORE_MAX: 70,
    40→  GO_MIN: 70,
    41→  GO_CRITICAL_MIN: 50,
    42→} as const;
    43→
    44→// ─── Seuils d'enrichissement (Contrat Questions Guidées v1.1, Section 3.1) ──
    45→export const ENRICHMENT_THRESHOLDS = {
    46→  AXIS_SCORE_MIN: 40,
    47→  GLOBAL_SCORE_MIN: 50,
    48→  GLOBAL_SCORE_MAX: 70,
    49→} as const;
    50→
    51→// ─── Limites d'enrichissement (Section 7.1) ──────────────────────
    52→export const ENRICHMENT_LIMITS = {
    53→  MAX_AXES: 2,
    54→  MAX_QUESTIONS_PER_AXIS: 3,
    55→  MAX_QUESTIONS_PER_SEQUENCE: 5,
    56→  TIMEOUT_SECONDS: 90,
    57→  TARGET_COMPLETION_SECONDS: 60,
    58→} as const;
    59→
    60→// ─── Plage d'ajustement de score (Section 8.2) ──────────────────
    61→export const SCORE_ADJUSTMENT_RANGE = {
    62→  SMALL: { min: 5, max: 15 },
    63→  MEDIUM: { min: 15, max: 30 },
    64→  LARGE: { min: 30, max: 50 },
    65→} as const;
    66→
    67→// ─── Score range ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→export const SCORE_RANGE = {
    69→  MIN: 0,
    70→  MAX: 100,
    71→} as const;
    72→
    73→// ─── Schema version ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    74→export const SCHEMA_VERSION = '1.0' as const;
    75→
    76→// ─── Enums canoniques ────────────────────────────────────────────
    77→export const IDEA_STATES = ['CAPTURED', 'ANALYZED', 'ENRICHED'] as const;
    78→export const DECISIONS = ['GO', 'EXPLORE', 'PARK', 'KILL'] as const;
    79→export const CONFIDENCE_LEVELS = ['LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'] as const;
    80→export const SEVERITIES = ['LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'] as const;
    81→export const TEXT_SOURCES = ['typed', 'voice_transcript', 'imported'] as const;
    82→export const LINK_TYPES = ['web', 'social', 'doc'] as const;
    83→export const FILE_TYPES = ['pdf', 'image', 'text'] as const;
    84→export const INPUTS_USED = ['text', 'voice', 'link', 'file'] as const;
    85→export const LABS = ['DISCOVERY', 'MARKET', 'STRATEGY', 'TECH', 'BUSINESS', 'NONE'] as const;
    86→export const EXPERIENCE_LEVELS = ['BEGINNER', 'INTERMEDIATE', 'ADVANCED'] as const;
    87→export const QUESTION_TYPES = ['single_select', 'multi_select', 'range', 'ranking'] as const;
    88→

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * K3RN.LABS — Calcul du score global (formule canonique)
     3→ * Source de vérité : Contrat Canonique Analyse Idée v1.1, Section 4.3
     4→ *
     5→ * Score Global =
     6→ *   (score_problem × 0.20) +
     7→ *   (score_market × 0.20) +
     8→ *   (score_differentiation × 0.15) +
     9→ *   (score_feasibility × 0.15) +
    10→ *   (score_business × 0.20) +
    11→ *   (score_founder_fit × 0.10)
    12→ *
    13→ * Arrondi à l'entier le plus proche, borné [0, 100].
    14→ */
    15→
    16→import { CANONICAL_WEIGHTS, ANALYSIS_AXES, SCORE_RANGE, type AnalysisAxis } from './constants';
    17→import type { AnalysisAxes } from './types';
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * Calcule le score global pondéré selon la formule canonique.
    21→ * @param analysis - Les 6 axes avec leurs scores
    22→ * @param weights - Les pondérations (optionnel, utilise les canoniques par défaut)
    23→ * @returns Score global entier dans [0, 100]
    24→ */
    25→export function calculateGlobalScore(
    26→  analysis: AnalysisAxes,
    27→  weights: Record<AnalysisAxis, number> = CANONICAL_WEIGHTS
    28→): number {
    29→  let weightedSum = 0;
    30→
    31→  for (const axis of ANALYSIS_AXES) {
    32→    const score = analysis[axis]?.score ?? 0;
    33→    const weight = weights[axis] ?? 0;
    34→    weightedSum += score * weight;
    35→  }
    36→
    37→  // Arrondi à l'entier le plus proche
    38→  const rounded = Math.round(weightedSum);
    39→
    40→  // Bornage [0, 100]
    41→  return Math.max(SCORE_RANGE.MIN, Math.min(SCORE_RANGE.MAX, rounded));
    42→}
    43→
    44→/**
    45→ * Vérifie que le score global fourni correspond à la formule canonique.
    46→ * @returns true si le score fourni est correct
    47→ */
    48→export function verifyGlobalScore(
    49→  analysis: AnalysisAxes,
    50→  providedScore: number,
    51→  weights: Record<AnalysisAxis, number> = CANONICAL_WEIGHTS
    52→): boolean {
    53→  const calculated = calculateGlobalScore(analysis, weights);
    54→  return calculated === providedScore;
    55→}
    56→
    57→/**
    58→ * Recalcule le score global après ajustements d'enrichissement.
    59→ * @param currentAnalysis - Axes actuels
    60→ * @param adjustments - Map axe → ajustement de score
    61→ * @returns Nouveau score global et nouveaux scores par axe
    62→ */
    63→export function recalculateAfterEnrichment(
    64→  currentAnalysis: AnalysisAxes,
    65→  adjustments: Map<AnalysisAxis, number>
    66→): { globalScore: number; updatedAnalysis: AnalysisAxes } {
    67→  // Deep copy
    68→  const updatedAnalysis: AnalysisAxes = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(currentAnalysis));
    69→
    70→  for (const [axis, adjustment] of adjustments) {
    71→    const currentScore = updatedAnalysis[axis].score;
    72→    // score_enrichi = max(0, min(100, score_initial + ajustement))
    73→    updatedAnalysis[axis].score = Math.max(
    74→      SCORE_RANGE.MIN,
    75→      Math.min(SCORE_RANGE.MAX, currentScore + adjustment)
    76→    );
    77→  }
    78→
    79→  const globalScore = calculateGlobalScore(updatedAnalysis);
    80→
    81→  return { globalScore, updatedAnalysis };
    82→}
    83→

Excellent! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report for you.

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE EXPLORATION: LABORATOIRE (LABORATORY) PAGES

---

### 1. EXISTING LAB DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Laboratory 1 - Discovery:**
- Path: `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/`
- Files:
  - `page.tsx` - Main page component (32 lines)

**Laboratory 2 - Structuration:**
- Path: `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/`
- Files:
  - `page.tsx` - Main page component (32 lines)

**Workspace Directory:**
- Path: `/src/app/workspace/`
- Files:
  - `page.tsx` - Main workspace/dossiers page (290 lines)
  - `[id]/page.tsx` - Generic project details page (33 lines)

---

### 2. LAB PAGE IMPLEMENTATIONS (WHAT'S ALREADY BUILT)

Both lab pages (`laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx` and `laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx`) are **IDENTICAL** in structure and use these shared components:

**Current Lab Page Layout:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         WorkspaceHeader (projectId)             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Dossier   │                                │ Expert
│ Panel     │      CanvasBoard              │ Chat
│ (Left)    │      (Center)                 │ (Right)
│           │                                │
│           │ - Grid background             │
│           │ - Sticky notes (draggable)    │
│           │ - Floating toolbar            │
│           │                                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Components Used:**
1. **DossierPanel** - Left sidebar displaying project materials
   - Shows source materials (texts, voice memos, etc.)
   - Shows research data (PDFs, images)
   - Analysis quality progress bar (static: 85%)

2. **CanvasBoard** - Center area for collaborative work
   - Grid pattern background
   - Draggable sticky notes (currently 3 hardcoded notes)
   - Floating toolbar with tools (Select, Note, Text, Shape, Pan)
   - Core Axiom, Opportunity, and Tags notes

3. **ExpertChat** - Right sidebar for AI expert consultation
   - Two expert avatars: Ava (Structure) and Marcus (Vision)
   - Pre-written messages
   - Chat input field

4. **WorkspaceHeader** - Top navigation
   - Back button to workspace
   - Project ID display
   - Status badge ("Analysing")
   - Last edited timestamp
   - Expert avatars
   - Language selector
   - Notification bell
   - User avatar

---

### 3. LAB NAVIGATION FLOW

**How labs are accessed:**

**Route A: From Project Cards → Detail Overlay → Lab:**
1. User clicks ProCard on workspace page (`/workspace`)
2. Opens `ProjectDetailOverlay` component
3. Overlay shows project analysis with smart action button:
   - **GO decision** → Routes to `/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/{id}` (Lab 2)
   - **EXPLORE/PARK/DRAFT** → Routes to `/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/{id}` (Lab 1)
   - **KILL** → Disabled (project rejected)

**Location in code:** `/src/components/workspace/project-detail-overlay.tsx` (lines 73-112)

**Route B: From Generic Project Page:**
- `/workspace/[id]` can also access the workspace layout (same as labs)
- This is a fallback route using `ProjectDetailsPage` component

---

### 4. RELATED COMPONENTS IN SRC/COMPONENTS/WORKSPACE/

**Files present:**
- `pro-card.tsx` (184 lines) - Project card component with score/decision display
- `project-detail-overlay.tsx` (431 lines) - Full-screen overlay showing project details + smart routing logic
- `project-folder.tsx` (117 lines) - Alternative 3D folder-style card
- `canvas-board.tsx` (120 lines) - Drawing/collaboration canvas
- `dossier-panel.tsx` (108 lines) - File/material management sidebar
- `expert-chat.tsx` (97 lines) - Expert consultation chat panel
- `header.tsx` (77 lines) - Lab workspace header with navigation
- `floating-dock.tsx` (168 lines) - Search/filter UI for workspace page
- `dossier-settings-dialog.tsx` (211 lines) - Settings modal (used in workspace)
- `workspace-layout.tsx` (43 lines) - Wrapper component
- `workspace-sidebar.tsx` (118 lines) - Alternative sidebar layout
- `monolith-card.tsx` (155 lines) - Alternative project display card
- `project-inspector.tsx` (163 lines) - Inspector panel for project details

**Note:** Most workspace components are built but not all are connected to labs. Canvas board and chat are currently **stub implementations** (comment on line 23 of both lab pages: "Lab X Mode to be implemented later if needed").

---

### 5. STATE MANAGEMENT & HOOKS

**Language/i18n System:**
- Location: `/src/store/language-store.ts`
- Provides: `useLanguageStore()` hook
- Wraps: `/src/providers/language-provider.tsx` context
- Dictionary: `/src/lib/i18n/dictionary.ts` (multilingual FR/EN/ES)

**Auth System:**
- Location: `/src/providers/auth-provider.tsx`
- Provides: `useAuth()` hook with session/user state
- Integrates with Supabase auth
- Profile auto-creation on first login

**No Lab-Specific State:**
- Labs don't have dedicated stores/state management
- Project data persisted in localStorage (`k3rn_projects`, `k3rn_active_project`)
- Workspace page manages all project state

**Other Hooks:**
- `/src/hooks/use-profile.ts` - User profile management
- `/src/hooks/use-speech-recognition.ts` - Voice input
- `/src/hooks/use-voice-input.ts` - Voice input wrapper

---

### 6. API ROUTES & SERVER ACTIONS

**Analysis API:**
- Route: `POST /api/analysis`
- Location: `/src/app/api/analysis/route.ts` (235 lines)
- Purpose: Canonical idea analysis via Claude API
- Inputs: Text, voice, links, files
- Outputs: Scores, decision (GO/EXPLORE/PARK/KILL), risks, levers, enrichment triggers
- Uses Anthropic Claude API (configured model: claude-opus-4-20250918)

**Enrichment API:**
- Route: `POST /api/enrichment`
- Location: `/src/app/api/enrichment/route.ts`
- Purpose: Enhanced analysis based on user answers

**Logo Generator APIs:**
- Multiple routes under `/api/logo-generator/` (not lab-specific)

**No Lab-Specific APIs:**
- Labs currently don't have backend integration
- All data is client-side localStorage
- No API for lab-specific operations

---

### 7. APP ARCHITECTURE

**Root Layout:**
- Location: `/src/app/layout.tsx`
- Providers: `LanguageProvider` → `AuthProvider` → `Toaster`
- Fonts: Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (monospace)
- Metadata: Title "k3rn.labs", Description "Institutional Ideation Platform"

**Middleware:**
- Location: `/middleware.ts`
- Purpose: Supabase session management
- Uses: `updateSession()` from `/src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts`

**Providers (Context/State):**
- `/src/providers/language-provider.tsx` - i18n context
- `/src/providers/auth-provider.tsx` - Supabase auth context

**Supabase Integration:**
- Client: `/src/lib/supabase/client.ts`
- Server: `/src/lib/supabase/server.ts`
- Middleware: `/src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts`
- Database tables accessed: `profiles` (user profiles)

**Core Pages:**
- `/` - Landing page
- `/capture` - Idea input page
- `/workspace` - Dossiers/projects dashboard
- `/workspace/[id]` - Generic project detail
- `/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]` - Lab 1
- `/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]` - Lab 2
- `/settings` - User settings
- `/logo-generator` - Logo generation tool
- `/canonical-demo` - Demo analysis

---

### 8. TECH STACK & DEPENDENCIES

**Core:**
- Next.js 16.1.6
- React 19.2.3
- TypeScript 5

**Styling:**
- Tailwind CSS 4
- CSS variables (custom theme system)

**UI Components:**
- Radix UI (primitives)
- shadcn/ui (built components)
- Lucide Icons 0.563.0
- Framer Motion 12.33.0 (animations)

**State Management:**
- Zustand 4.5.6 (for stores)
- XState 5.26.0 (state machines)

**API & Auth:**
- @anthropic-ai/sdk 0.74.0 (Claude API)
- @supabase/supabase-js 2.95.3
- @supabase/ssr 0.8.0

**Utilities:**
- Zod 4.3.6 (schema validation)
- Sonner 2.0.7 (toast notifications)
- Class-variance-authority 0.7.1
- Tailwind-merge 3.4.0

---

### 9. WHAT'S IMPLEMENTED VS. WHAT NEEDS TO BE BUILT

**FULLY IMPLEMENTED:**
✓ Lab routes and page structures
✓ Navigation from workspace to labs (based on decision)
✓ Workspace layout (3-panel: dossier, canvas, chat)
✓ Project detail overlay with smart routing
✓ Authentication system
✓ Project persistence (localStorage)
✓ Analysis API pipeline
✓ Decision logic (GO/EXPLORE/PARK/KILL)

**PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED:**
~ Canvas board (UI exists, but no real functionality)
~ Dossier panel (mock data only)
~ Expert chat (mock messages, no real AI chat)
~ Sticky notes (draggable, but no persistence)

**NEEDS TO BE BUILT:**
- Lab-specific UI/UX differentiation (Lab 1 ≠ Lab 2)
- Real canvas functionality (drawing, shapes, annotations)
- Real expert chat integration with Claude
- Project file upload/management
- Analysis visualization in labs
- Lab-specific workflows/modes
- Persistence of canvas work
- Collaboration features
- Lab completion/handoff flow
- Lab-specific APIs/endpoints
- Rich media support in dossier panel

---

### 10. PROJECT DATA STRUCTURE

**Projects stored in localStorage as:**
```javascript
k3rn_projects = [
  {
    id: string,
    title?: string,
    text: string,
    description?: string,
    color?: string,
    globalScore?: number,
    recommendation?: string (GO|EXPLORE|PARK|KILL),
    confidenceLevel?: string (LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH),
    viabilityScore?: number,
    classification?: string,
    timestamp: ISO string,
    isDemo?: boolean,
    isArchived?: boolean,
    analysis?: {
      axes?: [{ label, score, justification }],
      synthesis?: string,
      risks?: string[]
    }
  }
]
```

---

### 11. FILE PATHS SUMMARY

**Lab Pages:**
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx`
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx`

**Key Components:**
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/project-detail-overlay.tsx` (Lab routing logic)
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/canvas-board.tsx`
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/dossier-panel.tsx`
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/expert-chat.tsx`
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/header.tsx`
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/pro-card.tsx`

**Core Utilities:**
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/i18n/dictionary.ts` (translations)
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/analysis/` (analysis logic)
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/providers/` (context providers)

---

### SUMMARY

The K3rn.labs platform has a **solid foundation** for laboratory functionality:

1. **Routes exist** - Both Lab 1 (Discovery) and Lab 2 (Structuration) are fully routable
2. **Navigation works** - Projects route to the correct lab based on analysis decision
3. **Layout is built** - 3-panel workspace layout is implemented
4. **Components are ready** - UI components are in place but need real functionality
5. **Backend exists** - Analysis API is fully functional

**Main gaps:**
- Lab-specific differentiaton (both labs use identical code)
- Real canvas functionality and persistence
- Real expert AI chat
- File/material management
- Analysis result visualization in labs
- Project completion/handoff workflows

This is a **~30-40% complete implementation** of the lab system. The structure is solid, but the features need real logic and integration.

### 5. ANALYSIS & SCORING SYSTEM

**Analysis Architecture** (`src/lib/analysis/`)

**Constants** (`constants.ts`):
- 6 canonical axes: problem, market, differentiation, feasibility, business, founder_fit
- Canonical weights: Problem 0.2, Market 0.2, Diff 0.15, Feas 0.15, Business 0.2, Founder 0.1
- Decision thresholds: KILL <30, PARK 30-50, EXPLORE 50-70, GO ≥70
- Critical axes: problem, market, business (≥50 minimum for GO)
- Enrichment triggers: score <40 on axis or global 50-70

**Scoring** (`scoring.ts`):
- Global score = weighted sum of 6 axes, rounded to nearest integer, bounded [0, 100]
- Recalculation after enrichment with score adjustments (min ±5, max ±50)

**Types** (`types.ts`):
- AnalysisInput: meta, idea, inputs (text/links/files), context
- AnalysisOutput: meta, idea, analysis, scores, decision, risks, levers, enrichment, lab_orientation
- EnrichmentQuestion: id, axis, type, options, expected_impact
- EnrichmentResponse: status (completed/skipped), selected options, response time

**Decision Logic** (`decision.ts`):
- GO: global ≥70 AND critical axes ≥50
- EXPLORE: 50-70 OR critical axis <50 but overall viable
- PARK: 30-50 OR multiple weak axes
- KILL: global <30 OR critical axis <20

**Enrichment** (`enrichment.ts`):
- Detects low-confidence areas
- Generates guided questions to improve scores
- Applies adjustments and recalculates decision

**POST /api/analysis**
- Input validation against JSON Schema
- Claude API orchestration (claude-opus-4-20250918)

- Server-side score calculation and decision determination
- Enrichment requirement detection
- Returns AnalysisOutput with all metadata

**POST /api/enrichment**
- Two modes:
  - **generate**: Takes analysis + scores + decision → returns guided questions
  - **submit**: Takes responses → calculates adjustments → returns updated scores/decision
- Question generation via Claude
- Score recalculation with adjustment deltas
- Confidence level updates

**POST /api/logo-generator/** (Multiple routes)
- `/chat`: Chat with logo AI
- `/conversation/[id]`: Retrieve conversation history
- `/generate`: Generate logo from prompt
- `/refine`: Refine existing logo

- Client-side Supabase with SSR support
- Server-side Supabase client
- Middleware auth handling

---

### 9. LAYOUT & NAVIGATION

**Site Header** (`src/components/layout/site-header.tsx`)
- Variant support: "public" (landing) vs "app" (authenticated)
- Navigation items based on variant
- User nav integration

**User Nav** (`src/components/layout/user-nav.tsx`)
- User menu dropdown
- Settings, logout links

---

### 10. LANDING PAGE COMPONENTS (src/components/landing/)

- **Hero**: Main value proposition
- **BentoGrid**: Feature showcase grid
- **TrustedBy**: Customer logos
- **ImpactMetrics**: Key statistics
- **ExecutiveQuote**: Testimonial section
- **PricingMembership**: Pricing tiers
- **ProcessPipeline**: Workflow visualization
- **ProtocolFaq**: FAQ section
- **Footer**: Site footer

---

### 11. INTERNATIONALIZATION (src/lib/i18n/dictionary.ts)

**Supported Languages:** FR, EN, ES

**Structure:** Nested dictionary by feature area:
- common, auth, capture, settings, workspace, analysis, enrichment
- Each locale has full translations

---

### 12. TECH STACK SUMMARY

**Frontend:**
- React 19.2.3
- Next.js 16.1.6 (App Router)
- TypeScript 5
- TailwindCSS 4 with PostCSS
- Radix UI 1.4.3 (Headless components)
- Class Variance Authority 0.7.1 (Component variants)
- Framer Motion 12.33.0 (Animations)
- Lucide React 0.563.0 (Icons)

**State & Data:**
- Zustand 4.5.6 (Store management)
- XState 5.26.0 (State machines)
- Zod 4.3.6 (Schema validation)

**API & Backend:**
- Supabase (Auth + Database)
- Anthropic SDK 0.74.0 (Claude API)
- OpenAI SDK 6.18.0 (Legacy support)

**UI Features:**
- Sonner 2.0.7 (Toasts)
- Next-themes 0.4.6 (Dark mode)
- Simplex-noise 4.0.3 (Procedural generation)

**Testing:**
- Vitest 4.0.18

---

### 13. DESIGN PATTERNS

**Component Composition:**
- Composable card system with Header/Content/Footer slots
- Variant-driven styling via CVA
- Radix UI primitives wrapped with custom styling
- Slot pattern for flexible composition (asChild prop)

**UI Flows:**
- Modal dialogs for settings/editing
- Slide-over panels for inspector (responsive to drawer on mobile)
- State-based view switching (INPUT → ANALYZING → RESULTS)
- Progressive disclosure of information

**Animations:**
- Tailwind animate utilities (fade-in, zoom, slide)
- Custom keyframe: spin-rhythm for logo
- Framer Motion for complex sequences
- Sonner for toast animations

**Accessibility:**
- ARIA attributes throughout
- Focus management in dialogs
- Color contrast on brand elements
- Keyboard shortcuts (Cmd/Ctrl + Enter)

---

### File Paths (Absolute)

Key files referenced:
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/ui/` - UI library
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/globals.css` - Design tokens
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/components/workspace/` - Workspace UI
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/capture/page.tsx` - Capture flow
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/lib/analysis/` - Analysis engine
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/app/api/` - API endpoints
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/src/providers/` - Context providers
- `/Users/user/Desktop/K3rn.labas_test/k3rn-platform/package.json` - Dependencies

---

This is a sophisticated, well-architected platform with:
- **Enterprise-grade component library** with strict typing
- **Canonical analysis system** with JSON schema validation
- **Multi-language i18n** with context providers
- **Responsive design** with dark mode as default
- **AI-powered analysis** via Claude API integration
- **Rich enrichment workflows** with guided questions
- **Professional landing page** with engagement components

The codebase demonstrates strong patterns in component composition, state management, API design, and user experience flows.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for deploying two differentiated laboratories in the K3rn.labs platform. 

## Context
This is a Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind 4 + Supabase project for idea/project evaluation. The platform analyzes startup ideas across 6 axes (problem, market, differentiation, feasibility, business, founder_fit) and gives decisions (GO/EXPLORE/PARK/KILL).

Currently, Lab 1 (Discovery) and Lab 2 (Structuration) exist as identical stubs at:
- `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx`
- `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx`

Both use identical shared components: WorkspaceHeader, DossierPanel, CanvasBoard, ExpertChat. All are static mockups with hardcoded data.

## Requirements
1. **Differentiate Lab 1 (Discovery) from Lab 2 (Structuration)** with distinct experiences
2. **Real AI Expert Chat** connected to Claude API via `/api/expert-chat` route

## Architecture Details

### Lab 1 - Discovery (for EXPLORE/PARK/DRAFT projects, score < 70)
Purpose: Explore uncertainties, validate hypotheses, discover market insights
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data from localStorage (`k3rn_projects`), show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Analysis results: 6 axes with scores, weak axes highlighted
  - Risks identified
  - Decision + confidence
- **Center area**: Discovery workspace - NOT a canvas board. Instead:
  - Show the analysis verdict prominently
  - List of "Questions à Explorer" (generated from weak axes and risks)
  - Hypothesis cards that can be created
  - Key uncertainties to validate
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with discovery-focused expert persona

### Lab 2 - Structuration (for GO projects, score >= 70)
Purpose: Structure the validated idea into an actionable plan
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data, show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Strengths (high-scoring axes)
  - Levers to activate
  - Analysis synthesis
- **Center area**: Structuration workspace:
  - Business Model Canvas (simplified 4-block: Value Prop, Target, Revenue, Key Resources)
  - Action plan / Next Steps generator
  - Milestones timeline
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with structuration-focused expert persona

### Expert Chat API
- New API route: `POST /api/expert-chat`
- Uses Claude API (already configured in the project with @anthropic-ai/sdk)
- System prompt includes project context (analysis, scores, decision, risks, levers)
- Two expert personas:
  - Lab 1: "Ava" (Discovery expert) - asks probing questions, challenges assumptions
  - Lab 2: "Marcus" (Structuration expert) - helps structure, plan, define metrics
- Streaming responses for UX

### Data Flow
- Projects are stored in localStorage under key `k3rn_projects`
- When opening a lab, read project by ID from localStorage
- The project object contains: id, title, text, description, globalScore, recommendation, confidenceLevel, analysis (axes, synthesis, risks), timestamp
- Some projects also have `fullResult` which is the full AnalysisOutput

### Existing Resources to Reuse
- `/src/lib/analysis/types.ts` - Full TypeScript types for analysis
- `/src/lib/analysis/constants.ts` - Axis names, weights, thresholds
- `/src/components/ui/decision-badge.tsx` - Decision badge component
- `/src/components/ui/axis-score-bar.tsx` - Axis score visualization
- `/src/store/language-store.ts` - i18n support
- `/src/lib/utils.ts` - cn() utility
- Anthropic SDK already in package.json
- Sonner already available for toasts
- Framer Motion available for animations
- Lucide React for icons

### Design System
- Background: #22242B (canvas), #2F323A (card), #1A1C22 (recessed)
- Brand: #F75105 (orange), #F78E21 (secondary)
- Borders: rgba(255,255,255,0.06/0.10/0.18)
- Text: #F5F5F7 (primary), #A0A3AB (secondary), #6C6F78 (muted)
- Decision colors: GO=#22C55E, EXPLORE=#F59E0B, PARK=#6B7280, KILL=#EF4444
- Font: Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (mono)
- Radius: 6/8/12/16/24px

### File Structure Plan
Please design the detailed plan for these files:

1. **Modified files:**
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab1-specific components
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab2-specific components
   - `src/components/workspace/header.tsx` - Show real project title, lab name, decision badge
   - `src/components/workspace/expert-chat.tsx` - Real AI chat with streaming, project context

2. **New files:**
   - `src/app/api/expert-chat/route.ts` - Claude API endpoint for expert chat
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-panel.tsx` - Left panel for Lab 1 (replaces DossierPanel)
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-workspace.tsx` - Center area for Lab 1 (replaces CanvasBoard)

Design a detailed implementation plan for deploying two differentiated laboratories in the K3rn.labs platform. 

## Context
This is a Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind 4 + Supabase project for idea/project evaluation. The platform analyzes startup ideas across 6 axes (problem, market, differentiation, feasibility, business, founder_fit) and gives decisions (GO/EXPLORE/PARK/KILL).

Currently, Lab 1 (Discovery) and Lab 2 (Structuration) exist as identical stubs at:
- `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx`
- `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx`

Both use identical shared components: WorkspaceHeader, DossierPanel, CanvasBoard, ExpertChat. All are static mockups with hardcoded data.

## Requirements
1. **Differentiate Lab 1 (Discovery) from Lab 2 (Structuration)** with distinct experiences
2. **Real AI Expert Chat** connected to Claude API via `/api/expert-chat` route

## Architecture Details

### Lab 1 - Discovery (for EXPLORE/PARK/DRAFT projects, score < 70)
Purpose: Explore uncertainties, validate hypotheses, discover market insights
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data from localStorage (`k3rn_projects`), show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Analysis results: 6 axes with scores, weak axes highlighted
  - Risks identified
  - Decision + confidence
- **Center area**: Discovery workspace - NOT a canvas board. Instead:
  - Show the analysis verdict prominently
  - List of "Questions à Explorer" (generated from weak axes and risks)
  - Hypothesis cards that can be created
  - Key uncertainties to validate
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with discovery-focused expert persona

### Lab 2 - Structuration (for GO projects, score >= 70)
Purpose: Structure the validated idea into an actionable plan
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data, show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Strengths (high-scoring axes)
  - Levers to activate
  - Analysis synthesis
- **Center area**: Structuration workspace:
  - Business Model Canvas (simplified 4-block: Value Prop, Target, Revenue, Key Resources)
  - Action plan / Next Steps generator
  - Milestones timeline
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with structuration-focused expert persona

### Expert Chat API
- New API route: `POST /api/expert-chat`
- Uses Claude API (already configured in the project with @anthropic-ai/sdk)
- System prompt includes project context (analysis, scores, decision, risks, levers)
- Two expert personas:
  - Lab 1: "Ava" (Discovery expert) - asks probing questions, challenges assumptions
  - Lab 2: "Marcus" (Structuration expert) - helps structure, plan, define metrics
- Streaming responses for UX

### Data Flow
- Projects are stored in localStorage under key `k3rn_projects`
- When opening a lab, read project by ID from localStorage
- The project object contains: id, title, text, description, globalScore, recommendation, confidenceLevel, analysis (axes, synthesis, risks), timestamp
- Some projects also have `fullResult` which is the full AnalysisOutput

### Existing Resources to Reuse
- `/src/lib/analysis/types.ts` - Full TypeScript types for analysis
- `/src/lib/analysis/constants.ts` - Axis names, weights, thresholds
- `/src/components/ui/decision-badge.tsx` - Decision badge component
- `/src/components/ui/axis-score-bar.tsx` - Axis score visualization
- `/src/store/language-store.ts` - i18n support
- `/src/lib/utils.ts` - cn() utility
- Anthropic SDK already in package.json
- Sonner already available for toasts
- Framer Motion available for animations
- Lucide React for icons

### Design System
- Background: #22242B (canvas), #2F323A (card), #1A1C22 (recessed)
- Brand: #F75105 (orange), #F78E21 (secondary)
- Borders: rgba(255,255,255,0.06/0.10/0.18)
- Text: #F5F5F7 (primary), #A0A3AB (secondary), #6C6F78 (muted)
- Decision colors: GO=#22C55E, EXPLORE=#F59E0B, PARK=#6B7280, KILL=#EF4444
- Font: Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (mono)
- Radius: 6/8/12/16/24px

### File Structure Plan
Please design the detailed plan for these files:

1. **Modified files:**
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab1-specific components
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab2-specific components
   - `src/components/workspace/header.tsx` - Show real project title, lab name, decision badge
   - `src/components/workspace/expert-chat.tsx` - Real AI chat with streaming, project context

2. **New files:**
   - `src/app/api/expert-chat/route.ts` - Claude API endpoint for expert chat
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-panel.tsx` - Left panel for Lab 1 (replaces DossierPanel)
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-workspace.tsx` - Center area for Lab 1 (replaces CanvasBoard)
   - `src/components/workspace/lab2/structuration-panel.tsx` - Left panel for Lab 2
   - `src/components/workspace/lab2/structuration-workspace.tsx` - Center area for Lab 2
   - `src/hooks/use-project.ts` - Hook to load project from localStorage by ID

Provide a step-by-step implementation plan with critical details for each file.

- `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx`

- Font: Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (mono)
- Radius: 6/8/12/16/24px

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for deploying two differentiated laboratories in the K3rn.labs platform. 

## Context
This is a Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind 4 + Supabase project for idea/project evaluation. The platform analyzes startup ideas across 6 axes (problem, market, differentiation, feasibility, business, founder_fit) and gives decisions (GO/EXPLORE/PARK/KILL).

Currently, Lab 1 (Discovery) and Lab 2 (Structuration) exist as identical stubs at:
- `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx`
- `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx`

Both use identical shared components: WorkspaceHeader, DossierPanel, CanvasBoard, ExpertChat. All are static mockups with hardcoded data.

## Requirements
1. **Differentiate Lab 1 (Discovery) from Lab 2 (Structuration)** with distinct experiences
2. **Real AI Expert Chat** connected to Claude API via `/api/expert-chat` route

## Architecture Details

### Lab 1 - Discovery (for EXPLORE/PARK/DRAFT projects, score < 70)
Purpose: Explore uncertainties, validate hypotheses, discover market insights
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data from localStorage (`k3rn_projects`), show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Analysis results: 6 axes with scores, weak axes highlighted
  - Risks identified
  - Decision + confidence
- **Center area**: Discovery workspace - NOT a canvas board. Instead:
  - Show the analysis verdict prominently
  - List of "Questions à Explorer" (generated from weak axes and risks)
  - Hypothesis cards that can be created
  - Key uncertainties to validate
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with discovery-focused expert persona

### Lab 2 - Structuration (for GO projects, score >= 70)
Purpose: Structure the validated idea into an actionable plan
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data, show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Strengths (high-scoring axes)
  - Levers to activate
  - Analysis synthesis
- **Center area**: Structuration workspace:
  - Business Model Canvas (simplified 4-block: Value Prop, Target, Revenue, Key Resources)
  - Action plan / Next Steps generator
  - Milestones timeline
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with structuration-focused expert persona

### Expert Chat API
- New API route: `POST /api/expert-chat`
- Uses Claude API (already configured in the project with @anthropic-ai/sdk)
- System prompt includes project context (analysis, scores, decision, risks, levers)
- Two expert personas:
  - Lab 1: "Ava" (Discovery expert) - asks probing questions, challenges assumptions
  - Lab 2: "Marcus" (Structuration expert) - helps structure, plan, define metrics
- Streaming responses for UX

### Data Flow
- Projects are stored in localStorage under key `k3rn_projects`
- When opening a lab, read project by ID from localStorage
- The project object contains: id, title, text, description, globalScore, recommendation, confidenceLevel, analysis (axes, synthesis, risks), timestamp
- Some projects also have `fullResult` which is the full AnalysisOutput

### Existing Resources to Reuse
- `/src/lib/analysis/types.ts` - Full TypeScript types for analysis
- `/src/lib/analysis/constants.ts` - Axis names, weights, thresholds
- `/src/components/ui/decision-badge.tsx` - Decision badge component
- `/src/components/ui/axis-score-bar.tsx` - Axis score visualization
- `/src/store/language-store.ts` - i18n support
- `/src/lib/utils.ts` - cn() utility
- Anthropic SDK already in package.json
- Sonner already available for toasts
- Framer Motion available for animations
- Lucide React for icons

### Design System
- Background: #22242B (canvas), #2F323A (card), #1A1C22 (recessed)
- Brand: #F75105 (orange), #F78E21 (secondary)
- Borders: rgba(255,255,255,0.06/0.10/0.18)
- Text: #F5F5F7 (primary), #A0A3AB (secondary), #6C6F78 (muted)
- Decision colors: GO=#22C55E, EXPLORE=#F59E0B, PARK=#6B7280, KILL=#EF4444
- Font: Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (mono)
- Radius: 6/8/12/16/24px

### File Structure Plan
Please design the detailed plan for these files:

1. **Modified files:**
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab1-specific components
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab2-specific components
   - `src/components/workspace/header.tsx` - Show real project title, lab name, decision badge
   - `src/components/workspace/expert-chat.tsx` - Real AI chat with streaming, project context

2. **New files:**
   - `src/app/api/expert-chat/route.ts` - Claude API endpoint for expert chat
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-panel.tsx` - Left panel for Lab 1 (replaces DossierPanel)
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-workspace.tsx` - Center area for Lab 1 (replaces CanvasBoard)
   - `src/components/workspace/lab2/structuration-panel.tsx` - Left panel for Lab 2
   - `src/components/workspace/lab2/structuration-workspace.tsx` - Center area for Lab 2
   - `src/hooks/use-project.ts` - Hook to load project from localStorage by ID

Provide a step-by-step implementation plan with critical details for each file.

  - Risks identified

  - Show the analysis verdict prominently
  - List of "Questions à Explorer" (generated from weak axes and risks)

  - Milestones timeline

  - Lab 2: "Marcus" (Structuration expert) - helps structure, plan, define metrics

- Borders: rgba(255,255,255,0.06/0.10/0.18)

2. **Real AI Expert Chat** connected to Claude API via `/api/expert-chat` route

## Architecture Details

### Lab 1 - Discovery (for EXPLORE/PARK/DRAFT projects, score < 70)
Purpose: Explore uncertainties, validate hypotheses, discover market insights
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data from localStorage (`k3rn_projects`), show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Analysis results: 6 axes with scores, weak axes highlighted
  - Risks identified
  - Decision + confidence
- **Center area**: Discovery workspace - NOT a canvas board. Instead:
  - Show the analysis verdict prominently
  - List of "Questions à Explorer" (generated from weak axes and risks)
  - Hypothesis cards that can be created
  - Key uncertainties to validate
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with discovery-focused expert persona

### Lab 2 - Structuration (for GO projects, score >= 70)
Purpose: Structure the validated idea into an actionable plan
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data, show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Strengths (high-scoring axes)
  - Levers to activate
  - Analysis synthesis
- **Center area**: Structuration workspace:
  - Business Model Canvas (simplified 4-block: Value Prop, Target, Revenue, Key Resources)
  - Action plan / Next Steps generator
  - Milestones timeline
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with structuration-focused expert persona

### Expert Chat API
- New API route: `POST /api/expert-chat`
- Uses Claude API (already configured in the project with @anthropic-ai/sdk)
- System prompt includes project context (analysis, scores, decision, risks, levers)
- Two expert personas:
  - Lab 1: "Ava" (Discovery expert) - asks probing questions, challenges assumptions
  - Lab 2: "Marcus" (Structuration expert) - helps structure, plan, define metrics
- Streaming responses for UX

### Data Flow
- Projects are stored in localStorage under key `k3rn_projects`
- When opening a lab, read project by ID from localStorage
- The project object contains: id, title, text, description, globalScore, recommendation, confidenceLevel, analysis (axes, synthesis, risks), timestamp
- Some projects also have `fullResult` which is the full AnalysisOutput

### Existing Resources to Reuse
- `/src/lib/analysis/types.ts` - Full TypeScript types for analysis
- `/src/lib/analysis/constants.ts` - Axis names, weights, thresholds
- `/src/components/ui/decision-badge.tsx` - Decision badge component
- `/src/components/ui/axis-score-bar.tsx` - Axis score visualization
- `/src/store/language-store.ts` - i18n support
- `/src/lib/utils.ts` - cn() utility
- Anthropic SDK already in package.json
- Sonner already available for toasts
- Framer Motion available for animations
- Lucide React for icons

### Design System
- Background: #22242B (canvas), #2F323A (card), #1A1C22 (recessed)
- Brand: #F75105 (orange), #F78E21 (secondary)
- Borders: rgba(255,255,255,0.06/0.10/0.18)
- Text: #F5F5F7 (primary), #A0A3AB (secondary), #6C6F78 (muted)
- Decision colors: GO=#22C55E, EXPLORE=#F59E0B, PARK=#6B7280, KILL=#EF4444
- Font: Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (mono)
- Radius: 6/8/12/16/24px

### File Structure Plan
Please design the detailed plan for these files:

1. **Modified files:**
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab1-specific components
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab2-specific components
   - `src/components/workspace/header.tsx` - Show real project title, lab name, decision badge
   - `src/components/workspace/expert-chat.tsx` - Real AI chat with streaming, project context

2. **New files:**
   - `src/app/api/expert-chat/route.ts` - Claude API endpoint for expert chat
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-panel.tsx` - Left panel for Lab 1 (replaces DossierPanel)
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-workspace.tsx` - Center area for Lab 1 (replaces CanvasBoard)
   - `src/components/workspace/lab2/structuration-panel.tsx` - Left panel for Lab 2
   - `src/components/workspace/lab2/structuration-workspace.tsx` - Center area for Lab 2
   - `src/hooks/use-project.ts` - Hook to load project from localStorage by ID

Provide a step-by-step implementation plan with critical details for each file.

Design a detailed implementation plan for deploying two differentiated laboratories in the K3rn.labs platform. 

## Context
This is a Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind 4 + Supabase project for idea/project evaluation. The platform analyzes startup ideas across 6 axes (problem, market, differentiation, feasibility, business, founder_fit) and gives decisions (GO/EXPLORE/PARK/KILL).

Currently, Lab 1 (Discovery) and Lab 2 (Structuration) exist as identical stubs at:
- `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx`
- `/src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx`

Both use identical shared components: WorkspaceHeader, DossierPanel, CanvasBoard, ExpertChat. All are static mockups with hardcoded data.

## Requirements
1. **Differentiate Lab 1 (Discovery) from Lab 2 (Structuration)** with distinct experiences
2. **Real AI Expert Chat** connected to Claude API via `/api/expert-chat` route

## Architecture Details

### Lab 1 - Discovery (for EXPLORE/PARK/DRAFT projects, score < 70)
Purpose: Explore uncertainties, validate hypotheses, discover market insights
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data from localStorage (`k3rn_projects`), show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Analysis results: 6 axes with scores, weak axes highlighted
  - Risks identified
  - Decision + confidence
- **Center area**: Discovery workspace - NOT a canvas board. Instead:
  - Show the analysis verdict prominently
  - List of "Questions à Explorer" (generated from weak axes and risks)
  - Hypothesis cards that can be created
  - Key uncertainties to validate
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with discovery-focused expert persona

### Lab 2 - Structuration (for GO projects, score >= 70)
Purpose: Structure the validated idea into an actionable plan
- **DossierPanel**: Load REAL project data, show:
  - Project title, description, score
  - Strengths (high-scoring axes)
  - Levers to activate
  - Analysis synthesis
- **Center area**: Structuration workspace:
  - Business Model Canvas (simplified 4-block: Value Prop, Target, Revenue, Key Resources)
  - Action plan / Next Steps generator
  - Milestones timeline
- **ExpertChat**: Real AI chat with structuration-focused expert persona

### Expert Chat API
- New API route: `POST /api/expert-chat`
- Uses Claude API (already configured in the project with @anthropic-ai/sdk)
- System prompt includes project context (analysis, scores, decision, risks, levers)
- Two expert personas:
  - Lab 1: "Ava" (Discovery expert) - asks probing questions, challenges assumptions
  - Lab 2: "Marcus" (Structuration expert) - helps structure, plan, define metrics
- Streaming responses for UX

### Data Flow
- Projects are stored in localStorage under key `k3rn_projects`
- When opening a lab, read project by ID from localStorage
- The project object contains: id, title, text, description, globalScore, recommendation, confidenceLevel, analysis (axes, synthesis, risks), timestamp
- Some projects also have `fullResult` which is the full AnalysisOutput

### Existing Resources to Reuse
- `/src/lib/analysis/types.ts` - Full TypeScript types for analysis
- `/src/lib/analysis/constants.ts` - Axis names, weights, thresholds
- `/src/components/ui/decision-badge.tsx` - Decision badge component
- `/src/components/ui/axis-score-bar.tsx` - Axis score visualization
- `/src/store/language-store.ts` - i18n support
- `/src/lib/utils.ts` - cn() utility
- Anthropic SDK already in package.json
- Sonner already available for toasts
- Framer Motion available for animations
- Lucide React for icons

### Design System
- Background: #22242B (canvas), #2F323A (card), #1A1C22 (recessed)
- Brand: #F75105 (orange), #F78E21 (secondary)
- Borders: rgba(255,255,255,0.06/0.10/0.18)
- Text: #F5F5F7 (primary), #A0A3AB (secondary), #6C6F78 (muted)
- Decision colors: GO=#22C55E, EXPLORE=#F59E0B, PARK=#6B7280, KILL=#EF4444
- Font: Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (mono)
- Radius: 6/8/12/16/24px

### File Structure Plan
Please design the detailed plan for these files:

1. **Modified files:**
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab1-specific components
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab2-specific components
   - `src/components/workspace/header.tsx` - Show real project title, lab name, decision badge
   - `src/components/workspace/expert-chat.tsx` - Real AI chat with streaming, project context

2. **New files:**
   - `src/app/api/expert-chat/route.ts` - Claude API endpoint for expert chat
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-panel.tsx` - Left panel for Lab 1 (replaces DossierPanel)
   - `src/components/workspace/lab1/discovery-workspace.tsx` - Center area for Lab 1 (replaces CanvasBoard)
   - `src/components/workspace/lab2/structuration-panel.tsx` - Left panel for Lab 2
   - `src/components/workspace/lab2/structuration-workspace.tsx` - Center area for Lab 2
   - `src/hooks/use-project.ts` - Hook to load project from localStorage by ID

1→/**
     2→ * K3RN.LABS — Constantes canoniques pour l'analyse d'idées
     3→ * Source de vérité : Contrat Canonique Analyse Idée v1.1
     4→ */
     5→
     6→// ─── Axes canoniques ─────────────────────────────────────────────
     7→export const ANALYSIS_AXES = [
     8→  'problem',
     9→  'market',
    10→  'differentiation',
    11→  'feasibility',
    12→  'business',
    13→  'founder_fit',
    14→] as const;
    15→
    16→export type AnalysisAxis = (typeof ANALYSIS_AXES)[number];
    17→
    18→// ─── Pondérations canoniques (Section 4.2) ───────────────────────
    19→export const CANONICAL_WEIGHTS: Record<AnalysisAxis, number> = {
    20→  problem: 0.2,
    21→  market: 0.2,
    22→  differentiation: 0.15,
    23→  feasibility: 0.15,
    24→  business: 0.2,
    25→  founder_fit: 0.1,
    26→} as const;
    27→
    28→// ─── Axes critiques (Problème, Marché, Business) ─────────────────
    29→export const CRITICAL_AXES: AnalysisAxis[] = ['problem', 'market', 'business'];
    30→
    31→// ─── Seuils de décision (Section 5.1) ────────────────────────────
    32→export const DECISION_THRESHOLDS = {
    33→  KILL_SCORE: 30,
    34→  KILL_PROBLEM_MIN: 20,
    35→  PARK_MIN: 30,
    36→  PARK_MAX: 50,
    37→  PARK_CRITICAL_MIN: 25,
    38→  EXPLORE_MIN: 50,
    39→  EXPLORE_MAX: 70,
    40→  GO_MIN: 70,
    41→  GO_CRITICAL_MIN: 50,
    42→} as const;
    43→
    44→// ─── Seuils d'enrichissement (Contrat Questions Guidées v1.1, Section 3.1) ──
    45→export const ENRICHMENT_THRESHOLDS = {
    46→  AXIS_SCORE_MIN: 40,
    47→  GLOBAL_SCORE_MIN: 50,
    48→  GLOBAL_SCORE_MAX: 70,
    49→} as const;
    50→
    51→// ─── Limites d'enrichissement (Section 7.1) ──────────────────────
    52→export const ENRICHMENT_LIMITS = {
    53→  MAX_AXES: 2,
    54→  MAX_QUESTIONS_PER_AXIS: 3,
    55→  MAX_QUESTIONS_PER_SEQUENCE: 5,
    56→  TIMEOUT_SECONDS: 90,
    57→  TARGET_COMPLETION_SECONDS: 60,
    58→} as const;
    59→
    60→// ─── Plage d'ajustement de score (Section 8.2) ──────────────────
    61→export const SCORE_ADJUSTMENT_RANGE = {
    62→  SMALL: { min: 5, max: 15 },
    63→  MEDIUM: { min: 15, max: 30 },
    64→  LARGE: { min: 30, max: 50 },
    65→} as const;
    66→
    67→// ─── Score range ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→export const SCORE_RANGE = {
    69→  MIN: 0,
    70→  MAX: 100,
    71→} as const;
    72→
    73→// ─── Schema version ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    74→export const SCHEMA_VERSION = '1.0' as const;
    75→
    76→// ─── Enums canoniques ────────────────────────────────────────────
    77→export const IDEA_STATES = ['CAPTURED', 'ANALYZED', 'ENRICHED'] as const;
    78→export const DECISIONS = ['GO', 'EXPLORE', 'PARK', 'KILL'] as const;
    79→export const CONFIDENCE_LEVELS = ['LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'] as const;
    80→export const SEVERITIES = ['LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'] as const;
    81→export const TEXT_SOURCES = ['typed', 'voice_transcript', 'imported'] as const;
    82→export const LINK_TYPES = ['web', 'social', 'doc'] as const;
    83→export const FILE_TYPES = ['pdf', 'image', 'text'] as const;
    84→export const INPUTS_USED = ['text', 'voice', 'link', 'file'] as const;
    85→export const LABS = ['DISCOVERY', 'MARKET', 'STRATEGY', 'TECH', 'BUSINESS', 'NONE'] as const;
    86→export const EXPERIENCE_LEVELS = ['BEGINNER', 'INTERMEDIATE', 'ADVANCED'] as const;
    87→export const QUESTION_TYPES = ['single_select', 'multi_select', 'range', 'ranking'] as const;
    88→

- Lucide React for icons

- Background: #22242B (canvas), #2F323A (card), #1A1C22 (recessed)
- Brand: #F75105 (orange), #F78E21 (secondary)
- Borders: rgba(255,255,255,0.06/0.10/0.18)
- Text: #F5F5F7 (primary), #A0A3AB (secondary), #6C6F78 (muted)
- Decision colors: GO=#22C55E, EXPLORE=#F59E0B, PARK=#6B7280, KILL=#EF4444
- Font: Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (mono)
- Radius: 6/8/12/16/24px

   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-1-discovery/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab1-specific components
   - `src/app/workspace/laboratoire-2-structuration/[id]/page.tsx` - Load project data, use Lab2-specific components
   - `src/components/workspace/header.tsx` - Show real project title, lab name, decision badge
